title: Travelers (TRV) Q3 Earnings Miss, Revenues Beat Estimates

title: Travelers (TRV) Reports Q3 Earnings: What Key Metrics Have to Say
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Travelers (TRV) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended September 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Travelers 3Q profit falls due to elevated catastrophe losses
text: The Travelers Companies (NYSE:TRV) reported a 14% decline in quarterly profit for the third quarter due to elevated catastrophe losses stemming from severe wind and hailstorms across several parts of the United States. The company, based in New York, posted a core income of $454 million, or $1.95 per diluted share, compared to $526 million, or $2.20 per diluted share, in the same quarter the previous year.

title: Travelers (TRV) Q3 Earnings Lag Estimates
text: Travelers (TRV) came out with quarterly earnings of $1.95 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.93 per share. This compares to earnings of $2.20 per share a year ago.

title: Travelers Posts Wide Earnings Miss as Catastrophe Losses Expand
text: The property and casualty insurance company posts third-quarter core earnings of $1.95 a share, below forecasts of $2.99.

title: Travelers stock gains after profit misses expectations on higher catastrophe losses but revenue beats
text: Shares of Travelers Companies Inc. TRV, +1.02% edged 0.4% higher in premarket trading Wednesday, after the insurer reported third-quarter profit that missed expectations on higher catastrophe losses, while revenue rose above forecasts. Net income fell to $404 million, or $1.74 a share, from $454 million, or $1.89 a share, in the year-ago period.

title: Travelers (TRV) to Report Q3 Earnings: What's in Store?

title: Which Is A Better Pick – Honeywell Stock Or Travelers?

title: 3 Insurance Stocks for Long-Term Dividend Growth
text: Insurance stocks are attractive long-term investments for dividend growth investors. This is because the business model is appealing for investment.

title: Travelers Remains Attractive Despite Its High Catastrophe Losses
text: The Travelers Companies, Inc. has doubled the value of its shareholders' stake with a total return of 98% since 2015. Despite a 13% correction this year due to high catastrophe losses, investors should focus on the insurer's growth prospects and cheap valuation. Travelers is raising premiums rapidly in an inflationary environment and is expected to benefit from higher interest rates, as it is investing its incoming funds at higher yields.

title: Travelers: This Blue-Chip Insurer Is A Buy
text: Blue-chip insurance companies are a good investment opportunity in the current market environment. The Travelers Companies, Inc. is a reliable slow grower with an attractive valuation and a growing dividend. Despite a tough Q2, Travelers is expected to have a better Q3 and continues to see net premium growth.
